title: GPU Memory — The Four Major Components
description: Understand where your GPU memory actually goes: weights, gradients, optimizer states, and activations — the four components that determine whether your model fits.
tags: Weights, Gradients, and Optimizer States: Where Your Memory Goes
Activation Memory: The Hidden Memory Hog

notebooks.title: Memory Anatomy: Weights, Gradients, and Optimizer States
notebooks.objective: Calculate memory for model weights in different precisions, inspect Adam optimizer states empirically, and build a memory breakdown calculator for any model configuration.

notebooks.title: Activation Memory and Mixed Precision Training
notebooks.objective: Measure activation memory empirically across batch sizes and sequence lengths, verify the Megatron-LM formula, and implement mixed precision training with PyTorch's autocast and GradScaler.

notebooks.title: Will It Fit? A GPU Memory Calculator
notebooks.objective: Build a complete 'Will It Fit?' calculator that predicts total training memory for any model, generates compatibility matrices across GPUs, and recommends maximum batch sizes.

curator.name: Dr. Rajat Dandekar
curator.title: Course Instructor
curator.bio: Dr. Rajat Dandekar is a researcher and educator specializing in AI/ML, with a passion for making complex concepts accessible through intuitive explanations and hands-on learning.
